brief_justification: Correctly computes both conditionals (0.40 vs 0.309) and arrives at the right answer 'Yes'; only performs the surface-level arithmetic required, no exploration of the causal chain (education→skill→black hair) or second-order effects; proceeds linearly with no reflection on approach, assumptions, or potential pitfalls; derives P(HS)=0.55 as a basic complement check but performs no cross-validation or sanity check on joint probability bounds; notes 'in this hypothetical world' as a lone hedge but offers no structured uncertainty analysis or acknowledgment of modeling assumptions; considers only the direct comparison approach with no mention of alternative framings such as risk ratios or causal inference angles; every arithmetic step is shown explicitly with formulas and numbers, making the derivation fully reproducible.
